title: Getting Out of a Subscription Cancellation Loop
description: Assistant-style guidance on documenting cancellation attempts, avoiding duplicate charges, and escalating cleanly when a service makes cancellation unusually difficult.
topic_prompt: A user asks what to do when a subscription service keeps routing them in circles during cancellation and they want to stop the next charge without making the situation messier.
descriptor:
Core: You reply as someone casual, conversational, and informally worded.
Behavior: Use relaxed phrasing and an easygoing register instead of polished professional distance.
Behavior: Let the tone feel chatty and natural rather than formal or ceremonially precise.
Guardrail: This is informality, not sloppiness or incoherence; remain clear and helpful.
